gf.title: GitFlow Visualizer
gfv.request.environment.promote: Environment Promotion: dev → qa → staging → production
gfv.request.environment.hotfix: Production Hotfix: emergency fix on production
gfv.request.environment.rollback: Rollback: revert bad deployment on production
gfv.ui.log.title: Event Log
gfv.classic.feature.branch: Create branch {branch} from develop — isolates new feature development
gfv.classic.feature.auth_ui: UI work in progress — implementing login screens, validation, and CSS
gfv.classic.feature.auth_api: API work in progress — implementing authentication endpoints and JWT logic
gfv.classic.feature.tests: Running unit and integration tests for the new auth feature
gfv.classic.feature.pr: Create Pull Request !102 — requesting review from the core team
gfv.classic.feature.merge: Merge {branch} into develop — feature ready for integration testing
gfv.classic.feature.cleanup: Delete branch {branch} — cleanup after successful merge
gfv.classic.hotfix.branch: Create hotfix branch {branch} from main — emergency fix for production
gfv.classic.hotfix.fix: Critical bug fix — resolved memory leak in connection pool, verified by stress tests
gfv.classic.hotfix.deploy: Tag v1.0.1 on main — deploying fix to production immediately
gfv.classic.hotfix.develop_wip: Hotfix also merged back into develop — ensuring fix is in next release
gfv.trunk.direct.0: Developer commits feature A (search autocomplete) directly to trunk — no branch, no PR, small atomic change
gfv.trunk.direct.1: Bug fix committed directly to trunk — corrected off-by-one error in pagination, verified by existing tests
gfv.trunk.direct.2: CI/CD pipeline automatically deploys trunk to production — every green commit goes live immediately
gfv.trunk.direct.3: Another developer commits feature B (email templates) directly to trunk — independent small change
gfv.trunk.direct.4: Continuous deployment triggers again — trunk deployed to production, both features now live
gfv.trunk.short-lived.0: Create short-lived branch {branch} from main — expected to live only a few hours, not days
gfv.trunk.short-lived.1: Small, focused change — rename internal API method for clarity, update 3 call sites and their tests
gfv.trunk.short-lived.2: Merge {branch} back to trunk within hours — branch lifetime kept under 1 day as per trunk-based rules
gfv.trunk.short-lived.3: Delete short-lived branch immediately after merge — no long-lived branches allowed in trunk-based development
gfv.trunk.short-lived.4: Trunk deployed to production — change is live, continuous deployment handles the rest automatically
gfv.trunk.flags.0: Feature flag added to configuration (disabled by default)
gfv.trunk.flags.1: Dark-mode feature code committed, hidden behind flag
gfv.trunk.flags.2: Code deployed — feature invisible to users (flag off)
gfv.trunk.flags.3: Gradual rollout — flag enabled for 10% of users
gfv.trunk.flags.4: Full rollout — flag enabled for all users
gfv.trunk.flags.5: Flag and conditional logic removed — feature is permanent
gfv.trunk.flags.6: Clean codebase deployed without feature flag overhead
gfv.oneflow.release.0: Create branch release/1.0 from main — start of stabilization phase for upcoming release
gfv.oneflow.release.1: Stabilization commit — fix asset paths in staging configuration
gfv.oneflow.release.2: Second stabilization commit — update translations and API documentation
gfv.oneflow.release.3: Meanwhile, new feature lands on main — development doesn't stop during stabilization
gfv.oneflow.release.4: Rebase release/1.0 on top of main — moving release commits forward
gfv.oneflow.release.5: Fast-forward merge release/1.0 into main — maintaining perfectly linear history
gfv.oneflow.release.6: Tag v1.0 on main — marking the release point
gfv.oneflow.release.7: Delete branch release/1.0 — release complete, main remains the only long-lived branch
gfv.gitlab.feature.0: Create branch {branch} from main — isolating search development
gfv.gitlab.feature.1: Implement search index — mappings, analyzers, and indexing pipeline
gfv.gitlab.feature.2: Add UI components — dropdown, highlighting, and pagination
gfv.gitlab.feature.3: Open Merge Request !42 to main — requesting review from the backend team
gfv.gitlab.feature.4: MR approved and merged — search integrated into main and ready for promotion
gfv.gitlab.feature.5: Delete branch {branch} — cleanup after successful merge
gfv.environment.promote.0: New feature (support chat) committed to develop — ready for testing cycle
gfv.environment.promote.1: Second feature (analytics) committed to develop — batching changes for promotion
gfv.environment.promote.2: Promote develop to QA — merging accumulated features for testing
gfv.environment.promote.3: Deploy to QA — running regression tests and manual QA check
gfv.environment.promote.4: Promote QA to staging — tests passed, preparing for pre-release on production-like data
gfv.environment.promote.5: Deploy to staging — final verification of performance and DB migrations
gfv.environment.promote.6: Promote staging to production — release manager gives approval for rollout
gfv.environment.promote.7: Tag v2.0 on main — rollback point and official version marker
gfv.environment.promote.8: Deploy v2.0 to production — new features now live for all users
